title: Diagnosing unnecessary re-renders with React DevTools Profiler

performance_problem.symptom: List of 200 items lags on every keystroke in unrelated search box
performance_problem.metric_impact: INP 450ms (target <200); 200 components re-render per keystroke
performance_problem.business_impact: Users report typing feels broken; bounce rate +12%

diagnosis_steps: Open React DevTools Profiler, record session
Inspect commit phase; see all 200 ListItem renders
Identify: cause is parent context value reference instability
anti_patterns_to_avoid: React.memo on every leaf component (still re-renders when context changes)
useMemo with new object on every render
Splitting components without addressing root cause
reasoning: perf-001 treats Diagnosing unnecessary re-renders with React DevTools Profiler as a react_rendering bottleneck. The fix is valid only when React Profiler, browser INP trace, render-count assertions, and interaction replay show render_count_per_action (count) meeting the row target: only components with changed inputs re-render; correctness output must remain unchanged and the rollback path must be explicit.
before_metrics: perf-001 baseline for Diagnosing unnecessary re-renders with React DevTools Profiler: record render_count_per_action in count, sample at least five cold and five warm runs, keep the production-shaped fixture stable, and capture the user-visible symptom before changing code.
after_metrics: perf-001 optimized result for Diagnosing unnecessary re-renders with React DevTools Profiler: render_count_per_action must meet target (only components with changed inputs re-render), with no correctness diff, no adjacent-metric regression, and artifacts attached to the CI or review record.
trade_offs: Context split = more boilerplate; useMemo = subtle dep array bugs
regression_prevention: Render count assertion in tests (test-023); INP budget in Lighthouse CI
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive fix for Diagnosing unnecessary re-renders with React DevTools Profiler changes the nearest slow line without measuring render_count_per_action. It can move cost into another react_rendering boundary, hide cold-start or tail behavior, and leave the original user-visible symptom outside automated budgets.

title: Using EXPLAIN ANALYZE to fix slow queries

performance_problem.symptom: Order list query takes 2s on production data
performance_problem.metric_impact: Track db_query_p95_ms: EXPLAIN ANALYZE shows fewer rows scanned and p95 query latency inside the endpoint budget.
performance_problem.business_impact: If Using EXPLAIN ANALYZE to fix slow queries regresses, users see slower completion or retries; track support contacts, abandonment, and the technical metric together.

reasoning: perf-011 treats Using EXPLAIN ANALYZE to fix slow queries as a database bottleneck. The fix is valid only when EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS), pg_stat_statements, lock/connection wait telemetry, and a rollback rehearsal show db_query_p95_ms (milliseconds) meeting the row target: p95 query latency drops and EXPLAIN rows scanned decreases; correctness output must remain unchanged and the rollback path must be explicit.
solution:
-- before
EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS)
SELECT * FROM orders WHERE customer_id = 'abc' AND status = 'pending' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 20;
-- Seq Scan on orders (cost=... actual time=1800ms rows=18 loops=1)
--   Filter: ...
--   Rows Removed by Filter: 9999982

-- add compound index matching WHERE + ORDER BY
CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY idx_orders_customer_status_created
ON orders (customer_id, status, created_at DESC);

-- verify
EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS)
SELECT * FROM orders WHERE customer_id = 'abc' AND status = 'pending' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 20;
-- Index Scan using idx_orders_... (cost=... actual time=2ms rows=18 loops=1)
before_metrics: perf-011 baseline for Using EXPLAIN ANALYZE to fix slow queries: record db_query_p95_ms in milliseconds, sample at least five cold and five warm runs, keep the production-shaped fixture stable, and capture the user-visible symptom before changing code.
after_metrics: perf-011 optimized result for Using EXPLAIN ANALYZE to fix slow queries: db_query_p95_ms must meet target (p95 query latency drops and EXPLAIN rows scanned decreases), with no correctness diff, no adjacent-metric regression, and artifacts attached to the CI or review record.
trade_offs: Index adds write cost; storage; maintain on bulk insert
regression_prevention: Slow query log alert; query plan stability check
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive fix for Using EXPLAIN ANALYZE to fix slow queries changes the nearest slow line without measuring db_query_p95_ms. It can move cost into another database boundary, hide cold-start or tail behavior, and leave the original user-visible symptom outside automated budgets.

title: Partial indexes for selective queries

performance_problem.symptom: Active-orders query slow
performance_problem.metric_impact: Track db_query_p95_ms: EXPLAIN ANALYZE shows fewer rows scanned and p95 query latency inside the endpoint budget.
performance_problem.business_impact: If Partial indexes for selective queries regresses, users see slower completion or retries; track support contacts, abandonment, and the technical metric together.

diagnosis_steps: Index would be huge if all rows
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Index all rows when only few queried
reasoning: perf-025 treats Partial indexes for selective queries as a database bottleneck. The fix is valid only when EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS), pg_stat_statements, lock/connection wait telemetry, and a rollback rehearsal show db_query_p95_ms (milliseconds) meeting the row target: p95 query latency drops and EXPLAIN rows scanned decreases; correctness output must remain unchanged and the rollback path must be explicit.
solution:
-- only index active orders
CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY idx_orders_active
ON orders (customer_id, created_at DESC)
WHERE status = 'active';

-- query MUST include status='active' to use
EXPLAIN ANALYZE
SELECT * FROM orders WHERE customer_id='x' AND status='active' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 10;
-- Index Scan using idx_orders_active

trade_offs: Query plan must use the WHERE; queries without status='active' use other index
regression_prevention: CI stores an EXPLAIN/pg_stat snapshot for Partial indexes for selective queries; fail if db_query_p95_ms regresses, row scans/locks increase, or the rollback migration is missing.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive fix for Partial indexes for selective queries changes the nearest slow line without measuring db_query_p95_ms.
